summary: Removed the obsolete assembly file asm/sysdolphin/baselib/rumble.s after the rumble code no longer needed an asm fallback. The PR deleted 732 lines of PowerPC assembly covering HSD_PadRumbleOn and related unnamed rumble helper functions, reducing remaining assembly in sysdolphin/baselib. There were no review comments or PR body, so the exact preceding C replacement is inferred only from the title's statement that the file was now unnecessary.
what_changed: Removed 732 lines of PowerPC assembly from the sysdolphin/baselib rumble area.

The removed file contained HSD_PadRumbleOn plus multiple address-named helper functions such as func_803780DC, func_80378128, func_80378170, func_80378208, func_80378280, func_80378330, func_803783B0, func_80378430, func_80378524, and func_80378560.
The deleted assembly referenced HSD_PadLibData, lbl_804C22E0, OSDisableInterrupts, and OSRestoreInterrupts.
smart_moves: Removed the dead asm source once it was no longer needed, keeping the repository's asm fallback set aligned with currently unmatched code.
Used a narrow cleanup PR touching only the obsolete rumble assembly file, making the change easy to validate and merge.
Preserved project clarity by eliminating duplicate or superseded low-level rumble code instead of leaving stale assembly alongside decompiled code.
decomp_lessons: When a module reaches the point where its asm file is no longer required, delete the asm source promptly so future matching work can focus only on remaining gaps.
Small one-file asm cleanup PRs are useful after successful decompilation because they make progress measurable and reduce noise in the source tree.

For baselib work, confirm all symbols previously supplied by asm are now provided elsewhere before removing the fallback file; this PR's title indicates that condition was met, but the slice does not show the replacement code.
naming_conventions: The remaining evidence shows standard decomp placeholder naming for unknown functions, e.g. func_803780DC and func_80378430.
The one named public symbol in the deleted excerpt is HSD_PadRumbleOn, matching the HSD_* baselib naming style.
Data/global placeholders in the deleted asm used lbl_ address names such as lbl_804C22E0, while known baselib data used HSD_PadLibData.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: This PR is a cleanup after matching/decompilation rather than a matching tactic itself.
The deleted asm showed interrupt-guarded mutations using OSDisableInterrupts and OSRestoreInterrupts; C replacements for similar code should preserve those critical sections exactly.
The deleted routines manipulated per-controller rumble state indexed by a 0x0C stride and iterated over four pads, details that would have been important when matching the C implementation.
The deleted assembly contained linked-list/free-list style operations around HSD_PadLibData and per-pad queues; future rumble or pad decomp should preserve ordering and list updates carefully.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the PR slice.
No explicit reviewer rationale was available beyond the PR title indicating rumble.s had become unnecessary.
follow_up_queries: Find the PR or commit that added the C implementation replacing asm/sysdolphin/baselib/rumble.s.
Search for current declarations or definitions of HSD_PadRumbleOn and related rumble helpers in sysdolphin/baselib.
Check whether HSD_PadLibData and lbl_804C22E0 were later renamed or typed in headers after this asm deletion.
